name: Registry Browser
description: Public content browsing for a design registry. Search, filter, and explore patterns, themes, blueprints, archetypes, and shells.

pages.notes: Marketing landing for the registry. Hero with search, featured content grid, stats.
Public user profile showing published items and activity.

page_briefs.homepage: Registry landing with prominent search, curated featured content, and concise live registry stats. Search is still the hero, but the page should feel editorial and intentionally spaced at every breakpoint, with one consistent public-shell inset rhythm.
page_briefs.browse: Search-first browsing page. The filter bar stays responsive and high-signal: search remains full-width, type tabs can scroll, and secondary controls stack or collapse rather than squeezing. Cards emphasize title, description, and one trust summary instead of badge overload.
page_briefs.browse-type: Filtered registry view for a specific supported content type. Same responsive search/filter rhythm as the main browse page, pre-filtered to the selected type. Cards keep type identity without growing denser or noisier.
page_briefs.detail: Content detail page. Hero content is intent-first, with quick-start guidance and layered trust surfaces above the artifact viewer. Metadata chips stay subordinate to actions. The JSON/artifact surface keeps a padded frame and consistent x-axis rhythm.
page_briefs.profile: Public profile showing avatar, username, bio, and published item count with the same curated shell spacing as the rest of the registry browser. Published content and activity surfaces should stack cleanly and never feel like standalone mini layouts.

shells.top-nav-main: Top navigation bar with main content area
